quests.title: Who moors at the old quay?

objectives.text: Take a look at the old quay

start.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
start.text: You walked up the wrong side of the jetty. Everyone does, first time.

who.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
who.text: Marguerite, à Théodore. Thirty-one years keeping the Anse-à-la-Cabane light, so I notice what moves on this water. Lately something moves that shouldn't.

ask.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
ask.text: Somebody's been mooring at the old quay after dark. No lamp, no hull I know. My knees don't do wet planks anymore — go take a look for me?

thanks.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
thanks.text: North shore, past the fish shed. Mind the third plank. It lies.

refuse.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
refuse.text: Hm. The wind will turn you around eventually. It always does.

start.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
start.text: The quay. North shore, past the fish shed. My eyes are good, but they don't bend around corners.

start.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
start.text: So? Fresh rope on the old cleat, and the planks scrubbed where a keel came up. That's what you saw — I can read it on your face.

read.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
read.text: Then it's no ghost, it's somebody with a key to nothing. I'll ask Ovila whose skiff sleeps out. You did fine — for someone who walks up the wrong side of jetties.

start.speaker: Marguerite à Théodore
start.text: Ovila's nephew. Courting the Poirier girl across the baie, if you please. I watched the horizon thirty-one years for that.
